HERO.BADGE: NASEY OPTICA — Student Chapter of Optica in Cameroon
HERO.SUBTITLE: NASEY OPTICA is the local affiliate advancing optics and photonics research in Cameroon and Central Africa through student chapters, professional local sections, and educational outreach programs.

HIGHLIGHT.EVENT_TITLE: Frontiers in Photonics, Structured Light & Optical Communications 2026
HIGHLIGHT.EVENT_DESC: Join Prof. Kumar Vijay Mishra and leading researchers for FPSLOC 2026. A 3-day conference exploring applied photonics, nonlinear optics, AI, and featuring student poster sessions.

ECOSYSTEM.DESC: As part of the global Optica network, NASEY OPTICA operates through two officially recognized entities that advance optics and photonics in the region.
ECOSYSTEM.SECTION_DESC: Our professional network for doctors, teachers, engineers, and professors. Operating with regional scope, we drive high-level scientific exchange and research collaboration in Central Africa.
ECOSYSTEM.CHAPTER_DESC: Our student club at NASEY engaging students in optics and photonics through hands-on activities, workshops, and academic development programs.

PUBLISHING.DESC: Optica promotes research through its prestigious publishing platform, featuring world-class journals in optics and photonics.
PUBLISHING.FEATURE_1: Peer-reviewed journals covering all areas of photonics research
PUBLISHING.FEATURE_2: Open access options for maximum research visibility
PUBLISHING.FEATURE_3: Rapid publication times for cutting-edge discoveries

PROGRAMS.DESC: Optica offers comprehensive programs including scholarships, awards, conferences, workshops, and seminars to support students and professionals in optics and photonics.

OUTREACH.DESC: Optica encourages optics education and outreach programs for all social levels, from primary to secondary education, including disadvantaged communities.

MENTORSHIP.DESC: Optica fosters guidance of academic cadets by senior researchers and professionals throughout their optics and photonics journey.

OPTICA_CODE_OF_CONDUCT.TITLE: Optica Code of Conduct
OPTICA_CODE_OF_CONDUCT.SUBTITLE: Optica Anti-harassment Policy and Code of Conduct
OPTICA_CODE_OF_CONDUCT.INTRO: Organizers must publicly post a code of conduct and inform event attendees that they are expected to adhere to the highest professional standards. We offer the following language from the Optica Anti-harassment Policy and Code of Conduct as an example, but we understand that you may prefer to use your own wording.
OPTICA_CODE_OF_CONDUCT.TEXT:
Optica is committed to providing an environment that is conducive to the free and robust exchange of scientific ideas. This environment requires that all participants be treated with equal consideration and respect. While Optica encourages vigorous debate of ideas, personal attacks create an environment in which people feel threatened or intimidated. This is not productive and does not advance the cause of science. All participants in Optica and Optica-managed events and activities are therefore expected to conduct themselves professionally and respectfully.

It is the policy of Optica that all forms of bullying, discrimination and harassment, sexual or otherwise, are prohibited in any Optica or Optica-managed events or activities. This policy applies to every individual at the event, whether attendee, speaker, exhibitor, award recipient, staff, contractor or other. It is also a violation of this policy to retaliate against an individual for reporting bullying, discrimination or harassment or to intentionally file a false report of bullying, discrimination or harassment.

Bullying, discrimination and harassment of any sort by someone in a position of power, prestige or authority is particularly harmful since those of lower status or rank may be hesitant to express their objections or discomfort out of fear of retaliation.

Optica may take any disciplinary action it deems appropriate if, after thorough investigation, it finds a violation occurred.

RECENT.SUBTITLE: See what our community has been up to.

TESTIMONIALS.ITEM_1_TEXT: The mentorship program provided by the Local Section helped me secure a research position. The network here is invaluable.
TESTIMONIALS.ITEM_2_TEXT: NASEY OPTICA is driving real impact. Their workshops bridge the gap between theoretical physics and real-world photonics applications.

CTA.TITLE: Ready to illuminate the future?
CTA.DESC: Join NASEY OPTICA today as a member, or partner with us to support the next generation of scientists.

FOOTER.DESC: NASEY OPTICA — The official local affiliate of Optica (The American Society of Optics), advancing optics and photonics research and education in Cameroon and Central Africa.

ABOUT.SUBTITLE: Learn about our mission as Optica's local affiliate advancing optics and photonics in Central Africa.
ABOUT.MISSION_TITLE: Advancing Light Research in Cameroon and Central Africa
ABOUT.MISSION_TEXT: NASEY OPTICA is the official local affiliate of Optica (The American Society of Optics), based in the United States. We represent Optica's mission in Cameroon and Central Africa, promoting research in subjects related to light — optics and photonics.
ABOUT.MISSION_TEXT_2: Through our two officially recognized entities — the Central Africa Optica Local Section and the NASEY Optica Student Chapter — we advance scientific discovery, education, and innovation in light-based technologies across the region.
ABOUT.OPTICA_GLOBAL_DESC: Optica, based in the United States, is the American society dedicated to promoting optics and photonics research worldwide. Optica's global activities include:
ABOUT.OPTICA_1_DESC: Through Optica Publishing, the society promotes the publication of research works in prestigious scientific journals worldwide.
ABOUT.OPTICA_2_DESC: Comprehensive programs including scholarships, awards, conferences, workshops, and seminars for students and professionals.
ABOUT.OPTICA_3_DESC: Optics education programs for all social levels, from primary to secondary education, including disadvantaged communities.
ABOUT.OPTICA_4_DESC: Guidance of academic cadets by senior researchers and professionals throughout their scientific journey.
ABOUT.ENTITIES_DESC: NASEY OPTICA operates through two complementary structures recognized by Optica International:
ABOUT.ENTITY_SECTION_DESC: Our professional network for doctors, teachers, engineers, and professors. Operating with regional scope across Central Africa, we drive high-level scientific exchange and research collaboration.
ABOUT.LOCAL_LEADERSHIP_DESC: Meet the executive board of the Central Africa Optica Local Section.
ABOUT.ENTITY_CHAPTER_DESC: The NASEY OPTICA Chapter is a student chapter affiliated with OPTICA, the global scientific society dedicated to advancing optics and photonics. Based at the National Advanced School of Engineering (ENSPY, University of Yaoundé I), its mission is to promote research, innovation, and training in optics and photonics for students and professionals alike, through workshops, conferences, and projects — from telecommunications to medical technologies.
ABOUT.LEADERSHIP_DESC: Meet the executive board of the NASEY OPTICA Chapter.
ABOUT.LEADERSHIP_ROLES_DESC: The Supervisor and Advisor provide scientific expertise and guide chapter initiatives. The Chairman leads activities, supported by the Vice-Chairman. The Treasurer manages finances, the Secretary handles communication and documentation.
ABOUT.RESEARCH_DESC: Our chapter members have contributed significantly to the scientific literature with 9 peer-reviewed publications in 2024-2025.
ABOUT.VALUE_1_DESC: We uphold the highest standards of scientific inquiry and integrity in all our activities.
ABOUT.VALUE_2_DESC: We promote open access to knowledge and foster an inclusive scientific community.
ABOUT.VALUE_3_DESC: We build lasting connections between students, researchers, and industry professionals.
ABOUT.VALUE_4_DESC: We pursue excellence in every event, publication, and outreach initiative we organize.
ABOUT.MILESTONE_1: March 2023 - Official launch of NASEY OPTICA's activities.
ABOUT.MILESTONE_2: Official recognition of the NASEY Optica Student Chapter by Optica International
ABOUT.MILESTONE_3: Launch of the Central Africa Optica Local Section
ABOUT.MILESTONE_4: First joint conference organized with international keynote speakers
ABOUT.AFFIL_DESC: Both our entities are officially recognized by Optica, connecting us to a worldwide community of over 20,000 scientists, engineers, and entrepreneurs working with light.
ABOUT.PARTNERS_DESC: We collaborate with leading organizations to advance optics and photonics research and education in Central Africa.

RESOURCES.PUB_1: Aboukar, L. M. Mandeng, A. Mohamadou, C. Tchawoua, M. Youssoufa, and D. Djina, “Modulation Instability and Wave-Train Generation in Exponentially Saturable Nonlinear Positive-Negative Index Couplers,” The European Physical Journal Plus 141, 823 (2026).
RESOURCES.PUB_2: L. M. Mandeng, “Temporal Dynamics of Truncated Airy Pulses in Optical Waveguides under the Chen–Lee–Liu Equation” Journal of the Optical Society of America A 43, B92-B108 (2026).
RESOURCES.PUB_3: L. M. Mandeng and C. Tchawoua, “Hysteresis shape-like of the Airy pulse focusing area during its inversion induced by quartic dispersion” Springer Nature Journal of Optics, DOI: https://doi.org/10.1007/s12596-026-03052-y, (2026).
RESOURCES.PUB_4: L. M. Mandeng, Dieudonné Zanga, and R. B. Djob, “Quartic-Dispersion-Induced Modulational Instability in Coupled Nonlinear Chen-Lee-Liu Systems,” Chaos Solitons & Fractals 202, 117534 (2026).

RESOURCES.PUB_6: L. M. Mandeng and C. Tchawoua, “Using the time-transformation approach to generate free ballistic acceleration and inversion of the Airy pulse in dynamic optical media,” Wiley Annalen Der Physik 537, e00133 (2025).
RESOURCES.PUB_7: L. M. Mandeng, S. K. Boukar, and C. Tchawoua, “Asymmetric Airy pulse inversion in a semiconductor optical amplifier under third-order dispersion,” AIP Advances 15, 055327 (2025).
RESOURCES.PUB_8: L. M. Mandeng and C. Tchawoua, “Third-order and quartic dispersions inducing Airy pulse regeneration in optical fiber links,” Elsevier Physics Letters A 538, 130352 (2025).
RESOURCES.PUB_9: L. P. N. Tchawa, L. M. Mandeng, J. D. T. Tchameu, R. T. Menkem, F. K. Kom, J. L. Ntamag, and J. Pondi, “Heat transfer software for local materials in Cameroon (HTSLM 1.0): simplified building energy software based on local realities,” Springer Nature Discover Energy 5, 1-22 (2025).
RESOURCES.CORR_1: L. M. Mandeng and C. Tchawoua, Correction: “Hysteresis shape-like of the Airy pulse focusing area during its inversion induced by quartic dispersion,” Springer Nature J. Opt. (2026). https://doi.org/10.1007/s12596-026-03142-x
RESOURCES.CONF_1: L. M. Mandeng and C. Tchawoua, “Regeneration of truncated Airy pulses under alternating third- and fourth-order chromatic dispersions,” in Frontiers in Optics + Laser Science 2025 (FiO, LS), Technical Digest Series (Optica Publishing Group, 2025), paper JD1A.53.

PRIVACY.TITLE: Privacy Policy
PRIVACY.SUBTITLE: How we collect, use, and protect your data.
PRIVACY.SEC1_DESC: When you join NASEY OPTICA or register for our events, we may collect personal information such as your name, email address, institution, and professional background.
PRIVACY.SEC2_DESC: Your information is primarily used to:
PRIVACY.DATA_2: Communicate with you regarding upcoming events and opportunities.
PRIVACY.DATA_3: Improve our services and tailor our programs to the community.
PRIVACY.SEC3_DESC: We do not sell or share your personal data with third parties for marketing purposes. However, your data may be shared with Optica International for official membership validation if you apply for the Student Chapter or Local Section.
PRIVACY.SEC4_DESC: You have the right to access, update, or request the deletion of your personal data at any time by contacting us.

TERMS.TITLE: Terms of Service
